title: elaeocarpus stapfianus
intro: Elaeocarpus stapfianus is a plant species in the family Elaeocarpaceae, commonly known as the mountain blue fig, which is native to the mountainous regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, bark, and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Elaeocarpus stapfianus has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's parts are typically dried, powdered, and used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, or applied topically as a paste or ointment.
